revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sumiyoshi_Shrine_(Fukushima)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]住吉字住吉1 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 58 |緯度秒 = 28 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 52 |経度秒 = 58 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[表筒之男命]]
[[中筒之男命]]
[[底筒之男命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[景行天皇]]期 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月12日]]・[[10月13日|13日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、創建は[[景行天皇]]の御代に[[武内宿禰]]が勅命を奉じて[[東北地方]]を巡視した際、当地が海と陸の要害の地であり、かつ東北の関門にあたるので、航海安全と国家鎮護のために[[住吉三神]]を祀ったとする。 [[延喜式神名帳]]には[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一社として小社に列している。[[康平]]7年（[[1064年]]）には[[前九年の役]]にあたり朝廷から勅使が遣わされ、東国の賊徒討伐を祈願した。[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）には泉藩主[[内藤政晴]]によって社殿が改築され、現在の本殿はこのときに建てられたものである。 [[明治]]4年に郷社、[[昭和]]3年に県社に列した。 == 境内 == [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] 本殿は[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）の再建で、昭和33年[[8月1日]]に福島県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 関連項目 == * [[住吉神社]] * [[小名浜]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ 住吉神社]（公式サイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]住吉字住吉1 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 58 |緯度秒 = 28 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 52 |経度秒 = 58 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[住吉三神|表筒之男命・中筒之男命・底筒之男命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[景行天皇]]期 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月12日]]・[[10月13日|13日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、創建は[[景行天皇]]の御代に[[武内宿禰]]が勅命を奉じて[[東北地方]]を巡視した際、当地が海と陸の要害の地であり、かつ東北の関門にあたるので、航海安全と国家鎮護のために[[住吉三神]]を祀ったとする。 [[延喜式神名帳]]には[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一社として小社に列している。[[康平]]7年（[[1064年]]）には[[前九年の役]]にあたり朝廷から勅使が遣わされ、東国の賊徒討伐を祈願した。[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）には泉藩主[[内藤政晴]]によって社殿が改築され、現在の本殿はこのときに建てられたものである。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に県社に列した。 == 境内 == 本殿は[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）の再建で、昭和33年（[[1958年]]）[[8月1日]]に福島県指定重要文化財に指定された。 File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|神門 == 関連項目 == * [[住吉神社]] * [[小名浜]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ 住吉神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | address = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deities = {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7年|1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]]

It is an {{ill|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7年|1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]]

In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7年|1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7年|1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7年|1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18年|1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4年|1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3年|1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33年8月1日}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. Former {{ill|Ken-sha}} | established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|12=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=draft}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]]

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=draft}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|12=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Mutsu Province shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:住吉神社 (いわき市) up to rev 91346367
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]住吉字住吉1 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 58 |緯度秒 = 28 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 52 |経度秒 = 58 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[住吉三神|表筒之男命・中筒之男命・底筒之男命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[景行天皇]]期 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月12日]]・[[10月13日|13日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[小名浜]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、創建は[[景行天皇]]の御代に[[武内宿禰]]が勅命を奉じて[[東北地方]]を巡視した際、当地が海と陸の要害の地であり、かつ東北の関門にあたるので、航海安全と国家鎮護のために[[住吉三神]]を祀ったとする。 [[延喜式神名帳]]には[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一社として小社に列している。[[康平]]7年（[[1064年]]）には[[前九年の役]]にあたり朝廷から勅使が遣わされ、東国の賊徒討伐を祈願した。[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）には泉藩主[[内藤政晴]]によって社殿が改築され、現在の本殿はこのときに建てられたものである。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に県社に列した。 == 境内 == 本殿は[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）の再建で、昭和33年（[[1958年]]）[[8月1日]]に福島県指定重要文化財に指定された。 File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|神門 == 関連項目 == * [[住吉神社]] * [[小名浜]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ 住吉神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]]

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]]

Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわきの Municipal History]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわきの Municipal History]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]]

Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|36|58|28|N|140|52|58|E|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|qid=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|qid=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q63147962}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|qq=|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|qq=|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] {{wikidata link|Q63147962}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] {{wikidata link|Q63147962}}

[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
