revision.text:
[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * [[:en:Sumiyoshi Shrine|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q63147962]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.974444444444444|N|140.8827777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Sumiyoshi 1, Onahama, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Sokototsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto) | type = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}|established = Reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇|qq=draft}} | main_hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festivals = October 12–13 | prefecture= {{ill|Iwaki|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|住吉神社|すみよしじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} and is designated as a former {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} under the modern [[Shinto]] shrine Ranking system. == History == According to legend, the shrine was established during the reign of {{nihongo|Emperor Keikō|景行天皇}} by {{nihongo|Takenouchi no Sukune|武内宿禰}}, who was commanded to tour the northern provinces. He enshrined the {{nihongo|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} as deities of maritime safety and national protection, recognizing the strategic significance of the region as a key point of maritime and land transport. The shrine is mentioned in the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳}} as one of seven shrines in the {{ill|Ishikawa District, Mutsu|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Mutsu|1=Iwaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585428}}, categorized as a minor shrine. In {{nihongo|Kōhei 7|康平7th year |1064}}, during the {{nihongo|Former Nine Years' War|前九年の役}}, the shrine received an imperial envoy to pray for the subjugation of eastern rebels. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|寛永18th year |1641}} under the patronage of {{nihongo|Naitō Masaharu|内藤政晴}}, the lord of the Izumi Domain. In {{nihongo|Meiji 4|明治4th year |1871}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|Gō-sha|郷社}}, and in {{nihongo|Shōwa 3|昭和3rd year |1928}}, it was promoted to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} status. == Grounds == The main hall, rebuilt in {{nihongo|Kan'ei 18|1641}}, was designated as an Important Cultural Property by Fukushima Prefecture on {{nihongo|August 1, 1958|昭和33rd year 8月1日}}. File:Sumiyoshi Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Sumiuoshi Jinja Iwaki Shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) == See Also == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Onahama|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama|1=Onahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128846952}} == External Links == * [http://www.sumiyosi-jinja.jp/ Sumiyoshi Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[ja:住吉神社 (いわき市)]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (いわき市)|version=91346367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q63147962}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Fukushima_Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
